title: windows-source.json — transcribed Windows ranks, ratios (r_W) and dimensionless dispersions (d)
description: Schema for the Phase 8 hand transcription of the published Windows source runs (spec D18, harness-settings-and-validation.md 'Tooling'). STRUCTURAL RULE (D17.4): this file stores ONLY ranks, ratio-to-Heddle values quoted from each report's own ratio column, and dimensionless dispersions d — NEVER absolute times, point estimates, or any wall-time figure; validate.py additionally rejects any key containing an absolute-time token (time/ns/ms/mean/score/point/estimate/...). SR-1 NOTE: the transcription itself is deferred — no Windows protocol reports are published yet; this schema plus validate.py's structural checks ship first. Every transcribed value must carry a 'source' citation naming its file within the published Windows directory, and the transcription is spot-checked (>= 3 values per ecosystem) as a WI5 completion check.

sourceRuns.description: The published Windows report directories this transcription draws from (links/dates only — D17.1).

ecosystems.description: One entry per shipped ecosystem (Q7.2 subset semantics; absent ecosystems are simply absent).

harness.description: The harness whose native d-form (D15) the d values below carry.

dForm.description: The D15 d-form as transcribed, for the reader (e.g. 'StdDev/Mean').

windowsValue.description: One transcribed engine cell: rank + ratio + dimensionless dispersion + citation. additionalProperties:false IS the D17.4 structural weld — no absolute-time field can exist here.

r.description: Wall-time ratio to the same OS's Heddle anchor, quoted from the report's own ratio column — never recomputed from absolute times (D17.4).

d.description: Dimensionless relative dispersion in the harness-native D15 form.

source.description: Per-value citation: the source file within the published Windows directory.

heddleValue.description: The Heddle anchor row for the cell (r is 1 by construction on its own OS).
